summary: IDF eliminated Hamas financial operatives in Gaza, halting a half‑billion‑shekel transfer network (Source: @idf_telegram/24405). Israeli forces reported killing militants targeting Karmi Tzur and continue high‑readiness operations in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24411; @idf_telegram/24406). Hezbollah vowed to resist any Israeli presence in Lebanon while Iran accused the United States of aggression in the same theater (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4184; @rasedal3ado138e/372244).
key_points: IDF destroyed Hamas financial infrastructure in Gaza, stopping the transfer of over half a billion shekels to the group’s military wing (Source: @idf_telegram/24405).
Unit 636 intercepted and killed two militants launching fire‑bombs toward Karmi Tzur in the West Bank (Source: @idf_telegram/24411).
Israel’s chief of staff affirmed continued combat operations in southern Lebanon and high readiness for renewed fighting (Source: @idf_telegram/24406).
Defense Minister Israel Katz reiterated that Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4176).
Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem pledged the group will resist any Israeli presence in Lebanon (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4184).
Iranian advisor warned the United States bears responsibility for aggression in Lebanon and must be held accountable (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372244).
PressTV reported ten Palestinians, including a journalist, killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza despite a ceasefire (Source: @presstv/195056).

sections.executive_summary: IDF eliminated Hamas financial operatives in Gaza, halting a half‑billion‑shekel transfer network (Source: @idf_telegram/24405). Israeli forces killed militants targeting Karmi Tzur and maintain high‑readiness operations in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24411; @idf_telegram/24406). Hezbollah vowed resistance while Iran accused the United States of aggression in Lebanon (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4184; @rasedal3ado138e/372244).

points: Iranian delegation in Switzerland formally lodged a protest over recent statements by U.S. President Trump (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4183).
Iranian negotiating team announced a draft sanction‑waiver for Iranian oil, expecting issuance soon (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4185).
Bilateral talks between Iran and Qatar began, marking a diplomatic engagement (Source: @shin_persian/7152).

Iranian advisor stated the United States, under a bilateral understanding with Iran, is responsible for aggression and provocations by Israel in Lebanon and must be held accountable (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372244).
Iran warned the United States must immediately cease its activities in Lebanon, citing generous funding of proxy groups (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372244).
IDF announced the elimination of Hamas operatives overseeing a financial network that moved more than half a billion shekels to Gaza’s military wing (Source: @idf_telegram/24405).
Unit 636 intercepted and killed two militants throwing fire‑bombs toward the settlement of Karmi Tzur, neutralizing a third attacker (Source: @idf_telegram/24411).

Chief of Staff in southern Lebanon briefed division commanders, emphasizing continued fighting, protection of northern Israeli communities, and readiness for rapid escalation (Source: @idf_telegram/24406).
Defense Minister Israel Katz reiterated Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon despite diplomatic pressure (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4176).
Chief of Staff statements were repeated in a later briefing, underscoring high‑readiness and allocation of all IDF resources to the Lebanon front (Source: @New_security8200/202038).
PressTV reported ten Palestinians, including a journalist and four family members, killed in Israeli strikes across Gaza despite a ceasefire (Source: @presstv/195056).
IDF operations in Gaza targeted Hamas financial infrastructure, disrupting money flows used to pay militants and fund attacks (Source: @idf_telegram/24405).
IDF unit 636 neutralized militants attempting to attack the West Bank settlement of Karmi Tzur with incendiary devices (Source: @idf_telegram/24411).
Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem vowed the group will resist any Israeli presence in Lebanon, declaring the “eliminate Hezbollah” project a failure (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4184).
Hezbollah’s secretary‑general warned Israel will not remain in Lebanon and the organization will respond to any ceasefire violations (Source: @N12chat/213966).
Israel’s chief of staff confirmed ongoing combat operations in southern Lebanon and high readiness for renewed hostilities (Source: @idf_telegram/24406).
Defense Minister Israel Katz stated Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4176).
Iranian advisor blamed the United States for aggression and provocations by Israel in Lebanon, demanding accountability (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372244).
A massive fire erupted at the Ras al‑Khan gas complex on the outskirts of Doha, Qatar (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74187).
U.S. reportedly plans to allow Iran access to $6 billion of frozen assets held in Qatar for humanitarian purchases (Source: @VahidOnline/76561).
Iran‑Qatar bilateral talks commenced, indicating diplomatic engagement between the two Gulf actors (Source: @shin_persian/7152).
Iranian delegation protested in Switzerland over U.S. statements, reflecting broader regional diplomatic tensions (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4183).

key_developments: IDF destroyed Hamas financial operatives in Gaza, halting a half‑billion‑shekel transfer network to the group’s military wing (Source: @idf_telegram/24405).
Unit 636 killed two militants launching fire‑bombs toward Karmi Tzur, neutralizing a third attacker (Source: @idf_telegram/24411).
Israel’s chief of staff affirmed continued combat operations and high readiness in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24406).
Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem pledged resistance to any Israeli presence in Lebanon (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4184).
Iranian advisor accused the United States of aggression in Lebanon and demanded accountability (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372244).
PressTV reported ten Palestinian casualties in Israeli strikes on Gaza despite a ceasefire (Source: @presstv/195056).
A massive fire at Qatar’s Ras al‑Khan gas complex highlighted infrastructure risk in the Gulf (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74187).
sections.threat_assessment: Hamas financial networks in Gaza have been disrupted, reducing immediate funding for attacks, but the group retains operational capability. Israel maintains a high‑readiness posture in southern Lebanon, facing a determined Hezbollah that vows continued resistance. Iranian rhetoric escalates regional tension, blaming the United States for provocations and signaling potential proxy support, while the Gulf remains vulnerable to infrastructure incidents such as the Qatar gas fire.
sections.regional_response: Israel’s leadership publicly rejects any withdrawal from Lebanon, reinforcing its deterrence stance. Hezbollah’s leadership counters with vows of resistance, indicating a protracted low‑intensity conflict along the northern frontier. Iran’s diplomatic protests and sanction‑waiver negotiations reflect a dual strategy of political pressure and economic relief, while Gulf states grapple with security incidents and the prospect of frozen asset releases to Iran.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hamas financial channels should focus on residual networks after the recent IDF strike. Surveillance of Hezbollah activity in southern Lebanon must prioritize potential rocket or infiltration attempts following Israeli statements of readiness. Iranian statements suggest possible escalation of proxy activities; intelligence should track funding flows and militia movements linked to Iranian support. The Qatar gas complex fire underscores the need for infrastructure security assessments across the Gulf.
